% Dual-majority data macros: C++ majority vs.\ c-exact_std majority (1010 shared tasks).
% Needs \usepackage[table]{xcolor} and \usepackage{pgfplotstable}.
% Regenerated from analysis.ipynb -- do not edit. \input once, then cite \dualCExactStd... inline.
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTrueTrue}{205}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTrueFalse}{48}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTrueTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTrueUnknown}{308}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTrueTotal}{561}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdFalseTrue}{8}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdFalseFalse}{267}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdFalseTie}{2}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdFalseUnknown}{129}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdFalseTotal}{406}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTieTrue}{8}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTieFalse}{5}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTieTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTieUnknown}{17}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTieTotal}{30}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdUnknownTrue}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdUnknownFalse}{7}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdUnknownTie}{0}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdUnknownUnknown}{6}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdUnknownTotal}{13}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTotalTrue}{221}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTotalFalse}{327}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTotalTie}{2}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTotalUnknown}{460}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdTotal}{1010}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdAgree}{478}
\newcommand{\dualCExactStdDisagree}{532}

\ifdefined\dualcolorcells\else\let\dualcolorcells\relax
  % Square data cells: each cell is an explicit \colorbox of side \dualcellw -- a
  % fixed-width \makebox with a strut of the same height, no \fboxsep and no column
  % padding (@{}c@{}), so the shaded boxes are equal squares and tile contiguously.
  % \dualcellw auto-sizes to the widest header so nothing wraps.
  \newlength{\dualcellw}\settowidth{\dualcellw}{unknown}\addtolength{\dualcellw}{0.9em}%
  \newcommand{\dualbox}[2]{{\setlength{\fboxsep}{0pt}%
    \colorbox{black!#1}{\makebox[\dualcellw][c]{%
      \rule[-0.5\dualcellw]{0pt}{\dualcellw}\ifnum#1>50 \color{white}\fi #2}}}}%
  \pgfplotstableset{
    color cells/.style={
      col sep=comma, string type, column type={@{}c@{}},
      % Shade each count on the black! scale, normalised to the per-table max \heatVmax
      % (so the printed value is the raw count but the colour is 0..100). The shade is
      % computed here, at parse time, and baked into \dualbox as a literal.
      postproc cell content/.code={%
        \pgfmathtruncatemacro\dualshade{round(100*(##1/\heatVmax))}%
        \pgfkeysalso{@cell content/.expanded={\noexpand\dualbox{\dualshade}{\noexpand##1}}}%
      },
      columns/x/.style={column name={}, column type={r@{\hskip 0.6em}}, postproc cell content/.code={}},
    },
  }
\fi

\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \caption{Dual majority over the 1010 tasks shared by the original C++ suite and \texttt{c-exact_std}: rows are the C++ majority verdict, columns the \texttt{c-exact_std} majority verdict; cell shading is the count relative to the table maximum.}
  \label{tab:dual-majority-c-exact_std-macros}
  \def\heatVmax{308}%
  \setlength{\extrarowheight}{0pt}\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0}% rows tile: each \dualbox sets its own square height
  \pgfplotstabletypeset[color cells]{
x,true,false,tie,unknown
true,\dualCExactStdTrueTrue,\dualCExactStdTrueFalse,\dualCExactStdTrueTie,\dualCExactStdTrueUnknown
false,\dualCExactStdFalseTrue,\dualCExactStdFalseFalse,\dualCExactStdFalseTie,\dualCExactStdFalseUnknown
tie,\dualCExactStdTieTrue,\dualCExactStdTieFalse,\dualCExactStdTieTie,\dualCExactStdTieUnknown
unknown,\dualCExactStdUnknownTrue,\dualCExactStdUnknownFalse,\dualCExactStdUnknownTie,\dualCExactStdUnknownUnknown
  }
\end{table}
